summary: The gate is fail-open by construction and cannot self-block mindspec's own bead completes. (1) Registration requires a LITERAL panel.json: panel.Scan globs filepath.Join(root, "review", "*", FileName) where FileName == "panel.json" (panel.go:32,125). Mindspec's own ad-hoc verdict files (review/099-final-panel/verdict-*.json, the */<spec>-panel/*.json convention) do NOT match the glob — confirmed `find . -name panel.json` returns ZERO files repo-wide, while review/099-final-panel/ holds only verdict-adr-conformance.json + verdict-parity.json. No registration → no gate. (2) Fail-open traced end to end: panelGate (panel_advisory.go:149-153) calls panel.ForBead(panel.Scan(roots...), beadID); empty regs → `return nil, nil` (no error) BEFORE any fact-gathering, so complete.go:282 sees panelGateErr==nil and proceeds. ForBead additionally requires r.Err==nil && IsBead() && *BeadID==beadID (panel.go:154), so even a stray/non-bead/final-review panel.json (bead_id:null) never matches a bead complete. The pure decision mirrors this: PanelGateDecision returns Allow when Reg/Res is nil (gate.go:148). (3) This spec's own beads (pqju.1/pqju.2) completed THROUGH the gate precisely because fail-open held — there is no panel.json registering pqju.*, so Scan→ForBead→empty→nil,nil→complete proceeds; the gate is inert against mindspec's own dogfooding flow. (4) enforcement.panel_gate default is SAFE in both directions: DefaultConfig sets PanelGate:true (config.go:81), Load starts from DefaultConfig() then yaml.Unmarshal leaves absent keys untouched (config.go:155-156), so a MISSING key keeps the gate ENABLED (true) — and even on a config.Load ERROR complete.go:277-279 keeps panelGateEnabled=true. Enabled-by-default is the intended posture and is harmless here because fail-open dominates: enabled+no-registration still completes. (5) Malformed/partial panel.json fail-CLOSED (gate.go:156-160 Block on PanelErr/nil Panel; ResolveGateFacts:362-365 turns an unreadable dir into PanelErr) — this is DESIRED per ADR-0037 §6/§2 (a registered-but-unparseable panel must not tip fail-open) and is NOT a footgun for mindspec, because mindspec writes NO panel.json at all, so the malformed branch is structurally unreachable for its own completes; it only bites a project that actually registers a panel and then corrupts it, which is the intended hard-fail. The §6 asymmetry (no panel = open, broken panel = closed) is correct. No self-block path exists.
